properties.remark: Some tree and mostly minor structural damage was found along Grangeway Road. Two chicken houses were affected, as well as roofs on at least a couple of homes.
Video from a local firefighter shows a tornado near this location. Radar confirmed TDS was observed nearby as well.

Initial findings from tornado damage survey leads to a rating of EF-3 with peak winds of 140 mph. Additional surveys will be done in the coming days.

Initial finding from a tornado damage survey leads to a rating of EF-4 with peak winds of 170 mph. A well constructed home was completely destroyed. Additional information will be provided as surveys continue in the coming days.
Report of severe damage to home.
Trees down on AR Hwy 7 and Hwy 51.

Corrects time/date of previous tornado report from Cave City. Initial findings from tornado damage survey leads to a rating of EF-3 with peak winds of 165 mph. Additional surveys will be done in the coming days.

Homes damaged in the Jesup area from possible tornado.
Corrects previous tornado report from 2 W Black Rock. Roof blown off home just west of Black Rock near the intersection of US 63 and AR Hwy 117.
Roof blown off home just west of Black Rock near the intersection of US 63 and AR Hwy 117.
Ping Pong ball sized hail was falling in Black Rock per Emergency Manager.

Initial findings from tornado damage survey leads to a rating of EF-4 with peak winds of 190 mph. A well built home was totally detroyed, along with several vehicles that were rolled hundreds of yards. Additional information will be provided as surveys continue in the coming days.
ARDOT camera caught tornado looking north from the Exit 83 camera on I-57/US-67. Radar shows debris signature around Campbell Station.

Saline County Fire/Rescue communicated that a couple of very large trees fell on homes, just across the Saline county line into Hot Spring County. Trees fell on homes in the Traskwood area, leading to at least one entrapment yesterday. No casualties or injuries were reported as fire/rescue were able to free the woman from the tree entraped home. Thunderstorm wind damage consistent with a strong microburst is the cause of the damage.
